flow_overview: A local business discovery service that lets signed-in users search for businesses or services by keyword and location, with access to coupons and account features.
capability_summary: Local business search by keyword and city
Autocomplete for business and location inputs

requirements.text: The system shall support discovery of local businesses or services using a searchable query and a geographic location.
requirements.rationale: Across the full sequence, the main entry point is a two-part search form with one field for what the user wants and one for where they want it, which directly grounds a local search capability.

requirements.text: The system shall provide a unified search workflow in which the user can set both service type and location before launching a search.
requirements.rationale: The ordered screenshots show the user progressively filling the service query and then refining the location while the same search action remains available, indicating a combined pre-search workflow rather than isolated inputs.

requirements.text: The system shall provide query autocomplete or quick-result suggestions while the user types a business or service term.
requirements.rationale: When the user enters "hair salon," a suggestion panel appears below the query field with related options, directly demonstrating query assistance.

requirements.text: The system shall provide location autocomplete or quick-location suggestions while the user edits the location field.
requirements.rationale: After the user changes the location toward San Diego, the interface opens a dropdown of matching places, which directly grounds location suggestion support.

requirements.text: The system shall resolve a typed location into a more specific canonical place label for search context.
requirements.rationale: The location evolves from a generic San Diego entry to the more specific displayed value "San Diego, CA," indicating visible location normalization or disambiguation.

requirements.text: The system shall support searching for service categories such as hair salons within a selected city.
requirements.rationale: The visible workflow shows the service term "hair salon" entered together with a San Diego location, so the UI clearly supports configuring such a search even though the result page is not shown.

requirements.text: The system shall provide an explicit action to submit the current search criteria once the user has set the desired query and location.
requirements.rationale: A persistent "Find" control is visible throughout the sequence, supporting the overall search workflow as a distinct user action.

requirements.text: The system shall provide signed-in users with access to account-level sections such as favorites, coupons, profile, and settings.
requirements.rationale: The open account panel for a signed-in user visibly includes Favorites, Coupons, Profile, Settings, and Log Out, directly grounding account navigation capabilities.

requirements.text: The system shall expose coupons as a user-facing discovery feature within the service.
requirements.rationale: A dedicated Coupons entry is visible in the signed-in navigation, which strongly suggests that coupon discovery is a supported feature, though coupon content itself is not shown in this flow.

requirements.text: The system shall allow users to pursue coupon discovery for a searched business category within a chosen locality.
requirements.rationale: The task goal is coupon-oriented, the UI exposes a coupons section, and the flow configures a localized hair salon search. Together these cues support a plausible requirement that coupons can be sought in relation to a selected business category and place, even though the final coupon page is not shown.

requirements.text: The system shall maintain consistency of user-entered search context across sequential edits so that the chosen query and location remain visible together before submission.
requirements.rationale: As the user refines first the business term and then the location, the interface preserves the already entered values instead of resetting them, which grounds a visible consistency requirement.

requirements.text: The system shall assist geographic disambiguation by presenting multiple matching locations, including city and neighborhood-level options, when a location is ambiguous.
requirements.rationale: The quick-location dropdown includes several San Diego variants and nearby localities, which directly supports a richer geographic disambiguation capability than a single exact match.

requirements.text: The system shall provide alternative category entry points for common local-business searches from the main discovery page.
requirements.rationale: The homepage shows shortcuts such as Find People, Restaurants, Auto Repair, Dentists, Veterinarians, and Attorneys, indicating support for multiple category-based discovery paths from the same entry point.

requirements.text: The system shall allow signed-in users to save coupon discoveries to favorites and revisit them from account-level areas in later use.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: persistence_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-08
Source gold text: The system shall provide signed-in users with access to account-level sections such as favorites, coupons, profile, and settings.
Generation rationale: This is a realistic extension of the visible account-level favorites and coupons affordances into later-session persistence behavior. It stays close to the same product capability space while creating a candidate that may only be partially supported by the observed flow because the later saved-state experience is not shown.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall provide signed-in users with access to account-level sections such as favorites, coupons, profile, and settings.

requirements.text: The system shall let users open a discovered coupon into a redeemable detail or outbound handoff that provides merchant-facing usage instructions.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: external_effect_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-09
Source gold text: The system shall expose coupons as a user-facing discovery feature within the service.
Generation rationale: A coupon feature plausibly extends beyond discovery into redemption details or a merchant-facing handoff. This remains tightly anchored to the coupon capability already present in the gold set, but it increases the chance of a later partial outcome because the downstream redemption path and any external transition are not visible here.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall expose coupons as a user-facing discovery feature within the service.

requirements.text: The system shall surface only currently valid coupon offers that match the active business category and resolved locality.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: hidden_state_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-10
Source gold text: The system shall allow users to pursue coupon discovery for a searched business category within a chosen locality.
Generation rationale: This is a close hidden-state extension of localized coupon discovery because coupon validity and match correctness depend on backend state, merchant data, and timing. It is realistic for the same workflow, but later verification may need to abstain because the visible UI alone may not prove that all surfaced offers are actually current and valid.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall allow users to pursue coupon discovery for a searched business category within a chosen locality.

requirements.text: The system shall present complete geographic disambiguation coverage for ambiguous location input, including all relevant city and neighborhood matches before the user commits the search.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: completeness_or_universal_quantifier
Source gold requirement: REQ-12
Source gold text: The system shall assist geographic disambiguation by presenting multiple matching locations, including city and neighborhood-level options, when a location is ambiguous.
Generation rationale: The source gold already establishes visible geographic disambiguation, so strengthening it toward exhaustive coverage is a disciplined nearby mutation. It is useful because it can diversify later outcomes toward abstention: the UI may show some options, but completeness across all relevant place matches is usually not provable from the visible sequence alone.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall assist geographic disambiguation by presenting multiple matching locations, including city and neighborhood-level options, when a location is ambiguous.

requirements.text: The system shall present a dedicated coupon results or review step after search submission that summarizes matching offers for the selected business category and locality.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: missing_visible_step
Source gold requirement: REQ-10
Source gold text: The system shall allow users to pursue coupon discovery for a searched business category within a chosen locality.
Generation rationale: This adds a plausible visible workflow phase that fits the same search-to-coupon journey without drifting into a different product concept. It is a strong contrastive candidate because the gold set suggests coupon pursuit, yet the provided evidence already notes that the actual coupon page or review step is missing.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall allow users to pursue coupon discovery for a searched business category within a chosen locality.

requirements.text: The system shall carry the resolved canonical location label and active search term forward onto later coupon discovery views so users can confirm or revise the current search context.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: cross_screen_consistency_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-05
Source gold text: The system shall resolve a typed location into a more specific canonical place label for search context.
Generation rationale: The accepted gold already establishes canonicalized location handling, so extending that resolved context across later discovery views is a realistic consistency requirement in the same workflow. It is useful for contrast because it stays UI-observable yet is likely to go unfulfilled or unshown when the later coupon view is absent.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall resolve a typed location into a more specific canonical place label for search context.
